name: gemini-api-local
description: Local Gemini API configuration for the OCR app. The API key is loaded from GEMINI_API_KEY in .env; do not hardcode secrets here.
notes: This is an MCP-style local config reference for agents/tools.
Actual OCR app usage is implemented in app/ocr/ and app/llm/.
Normal uploads use Google Vision OCR first, then Gemini text-only sorting.
Multiple Gemini API keys can be configured, but Gemini API rate limits are project-level, not key-level.
The app tracks local Google Vision OCR units and estimated cost; authoritative billing lives in Google Cloud Console.
Use Google AI Studio to view authoritative project-level rate limits and quota.

business_card_extraction_guidance.front_side_priority: Use the front side as the primary source for person name, designation, company/business, email, website, and direct contact numbers.
runtime_pipeline: Google Vision DOCUMENT_TEXT_DETECTION extracts OCR text from the front and optional back image.
The app stores raw OCR results before calling Gemini.
Gemini receives OCR text and rule-based candidate hints only during normal upload processing.
Gemini must sort the OCR transcript into Excel fields; it must not invent values missing from OCR text.
text_sorting: Preserve front_text, back_text, and all_visible_text exactly from OCR input.
Map fields only from that OCR transcript.
Return field_evidence and uncertain_fields for audit.
If a field cannot be supported by an OCR line, return null instead of guessing.
Prefer blank/null over a guessed value. Accuracy is more important than filling every cell.
company_detection: The company/business name is usually the top-most stylized logo or brand text on the front side.
The logo may be top-left or top-right and may be graphic/stylized rather than normal OCR body text.
Do not use branch names, office labels, building names, slogans, service-list headings, address landmarks, or back-side product lists as the company name.
back_side_handling: Back sides often contain branch addresses, support facilities, product/service outlines, product photos, safety slogans, and we-sell lists.
Use back-side text for address and category support.
Do not replace front-side contact details with back-side branch-office numbers unless the front side is missing that field.
ignore_visual_noise: Ignore QR codes, decorative icons, certification icons, product photos, separators, and handwritten notes unless printed text beside them is a contact field.
validation_rules: Email must contain @ and a valid domain.
Website must be a printed domain or URL, not guessed from email unless separately printed.
The app validates Email1/Email2, Website, and Contact1-3 from the transcript after Gemini returns. Unsupported values are removed.
For country_code, explicit phone prefixes such as +62, +91, and +971 win over address guesses.
Contact1, Contact2, and Contact3 must be digits only and include country calling code when visible or inferable.
Do not include plus signs, spaces, hyphens, or parentheses in Contact1/Contact2/Contact3.
If Contact1/Contact2/Contact3 already starts with the country calling code, do not add the code again.
If multiple card sides are provided, use front side for person/company/contact fields and back side for address/category/supporting details.
